sanskrit:
नियतं सङ्गरहितमरागद्वेषतः कृतम्।
अफलप्रेप्सुना कर्म यत्तत्सात्त्विकमुच्यते॥
transliteration:
niyataṃ saṅga-rahitam arāga-dveṣataḥ kṛtam
aphala-prepsunā karma yat tat sāttvikam ucyate
literal_meaning: That action which is regulated, free from attachment, devoid of love or hatred, performed without desire for results—such action is declared to be in the mode of goodness.
contextual_meaning: Healthy action is duty-bound, emotionally neutral, and intrinsically motivated; it protects the ego from both grandiosity and shame by removing the scoreboard.
psychological_state: Flow-state: focused, non-attached, values-aligned behavior that lowers amygdala reactivity and increases self-efficacy.
trigger_scenario: A nurse works a 12-hour shift, follows protocol, feels no hatred toward difficult patients, no favoritism toward charming ones, and clocks out without counting ‘likes’ or overtime pay as the measure of worth.
anchor_text: Do your duty calmly, without clinging to success or fearing failure.
